title: $textexist
text: $textexist Return an indication of whether the specified message or help text exists. $textexist ( IDString ) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description IDString String Identifier of the message or help text Return Values 1 , if the message or help text identified by IDString exists. 0 , otherwise. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The $textexist function indicates whether the message or help text specified by IDString can be found in UAR files containing /msg/*.msg files. It is commonly used to determine if a message or help text exists before trying to display it on a form, but it can be used in a report or service as well. $textexist uses the current language and library (in $language and $variation ) to retrieve the text. If a message or help text cannot be found with the current $language and $variation , Uniface also looks for combinations with language USA and library USYS. Example: Initializing a Static Label Field In the following example, the function $textexist is used to initialize a static label field, ensuring that the 8006 message does not appear in the label: ; initialize labels only when message exists if ($textexist("%%$componentname%%%.LAB1")) LAB1/init = $text("%%$componentname%%%.LAB1") else ; label not found, use default LAB1/init = "Default Label" endif Copy Related Links $text Return the text of a message or help text.
